changes: No newer Tier 1 release landed between the 2026-04-06 and 2026-04-07 UTC scans; the biggest fresh product signal in the current window is still OpenClaw v2026.4.5, published on 2026-04-06.
SwarmClaw still looks unusually active in the orchestration lane, with its 2026-04-06 release burst now carrying into today's scan as a live signal rather than a one-off blip.
The atlas dataset itself materially improved today: Hermes Agent is now pinned to its canonical repo, NousResearch/hermes-agent, while TinyClaw and HiClaw now point to the correct official repos.

The scan also promoted several newly source-backed OpenClaw showcase use cases into the research layer: device-control handoff / home automation, scheduled visual morning briefings, and accounting intake from email plus PDFs.
whyItMatters: The product market read did not meaningfully shift overnight; today's value is confirmation and cleanup, not invented movement.
OpenClaw's 2026-04-06 release still anchors the category, which matters more than derivative churn by itself.
The canonical repo corrections matter because the hosted atlas should track the real official surfaces, not stale aliases or superseded repos.

The new showcase-backed use cases matter because they broaden the evidence base for how OpenClaw is actually being used: not just chat and coding, but steady-state automation and back-office workflow compression.
This remains meaningful but incremental: enough for a short operator digest, but not enough to rewrite the taxonomy or executive view.
